sections.problem_statement: Users need to schedule tasks that repeat on a regular basis. Currently, they must manually duplicate tasks every week or month, which is time-consuming and error-prone.
user_stories: As a project manager, I want to create recurring weekly tasks so that standups and check-ins are scheduled automatically.
As a team member, I want recurring tasks to appear in my task list on the correct due date without manual intervention.
success_metrics: Recurring task feature adoption rate > 30% of active users within 60 days
Reduction in manually duplicated tasks by 40% (measured via duplicate detection heuristic)
NPS score for task management sub-experience increases by 5 points
scope_in: Weekly recurrence (every N weeks, specific day of week)
Monthly recurrence (on a specific date or day of month)
Option to set an end date or max occurrence count
scope_out: Recurrence for projects (only tasks in scope)
open_questions: What happens to a recurring task if the original is deleted?
How should subtasks of a recurring parent task be handled?
sections.technical_considerations: Backend will need a scheduled job to create task instances. Estimated at 3 engineering weeks. Database schema change required.
sections.launch_plan: Beta with 5 volunteer customers in week 8. GA in week 10 with in-app tooltip announcement.
